category_title: Students with or at-risk for EBD who engage in disruptive behaviors such as wandering and bothering peers
minimal_context: The student is presented with new reading material during general classroom instruction.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We are going to look at some new material in reading today. Please look at the instructional materials on your desk.

STUDENT: (Gets out of seat without permission but not bothering anyone else)

TUTOR: Please return to your seat and focus on the reading.

STUDENT: (Sits down, looking away from the teacher and instructional materials and directing attention toward students playing outside the window)

TUTOR: You need to pay attention to the lesson.

STUDENT: (Starts texting a friend, not engaged in active or passive engagement but engaging in an activity that is incompatible with any assigned task)

options.A: Devote a classroom bulletin board to reading recommendations with slips available for students.
options.B: Increase the time spent teaching as opposed to not teaching during the reading lesson.
options.C: Introduce the student to reading materials that are of an appropriate reading and engagement level.
options.D: Create a reading community founded on respect for student choice and individual perspectives.
